text: The aerobic-anaerobic threshold corresponds to a blood lactate concentration of 4 mmol/l.

When exercising at constant speed corresponding to the aerobic-anaerobic threshold, blood lactate rises initially to approximately 4 mmol/l and then stabilizes, indicating a maximal lactate steady state.
Heart rate at the aerobic-anaerobic threshold (4 mmol/l lactate) averages above 170 beats per minute in elite endurance athletes.
During sustained exercise at the aerobic-anaerobic threshold, heart rate displays a slight but continuous upward drift even when running speed is held constant.
Endurance training intensity can be optimally regulated by monitoring heart rate relative to individually determined aerobic and anaerobic thresholds.
Elite endurance athletes can sustain exercise at the 4 mmol/l lactate threshold for at least 30 minutes, confirming it as a viable steady-state training intensity.
